zh.lifeSummary: Mabel de Clare於1148年出生在英國林可爾史爾。她於大約1170年與Nigel de Mowbray結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1204年在英國諾福爾可布雷可藍德享年56歲，埋葬在英國越可爾可沃爾德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mabel de Clare于1148年出生在英国林可爾史爾。她于大约1170年与Nigel de Mowbray结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1204年在英国諾福爾可布雷可藍德享年56岁，埋葬在英国越可爾可沃爾德。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: Mabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a nickname from the Old French vocabulary word amabel, amable ‘lovely’ (akin to modern English amiable ‘friendly, good-humoured’). The initial vowel began to be lost as early as the 12th century (the same woman is referred to as both Mabilia and Amabilia in a document of 1185 ), but a short vowel in the resulting first syllable was standard, giving a rhyme with babble, until the 19th century, when people began to pronounce the name to rhyme with table.

en.lifeSummary: Mabel de Clare was born in 1148, in Lincolnshire, England. She married Nigel de Mowbray about 1170. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1204, in Breckland, Norfolk, England, at the age of 56, and was buried in Coxwold, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Mabel de Clare est née en 1148, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Nigel de Mowbray environ 1170. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1204, à Breckland, Norfolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 56 ans, et a été enterrée à Coxwold, Everwyck, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Mabel de Clare wurde 1148 in Lincolnshire, England geboren. Sie hat um 1170 Nigel de Mowbray geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1204 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Breckland, Norfolk, England und wurde in Coxwold, Yorkshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Mabel de Clare è nata il nel 1148 nel Lincolnshire, England. Lei sposato Nigel de Mowbray circa 1170. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1204, nel Breckland, Norfolk, England, all'età di 56 anni, e fu sepolta a Coxwold, Yorkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Mabel de Clareは1148年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアで生まれた。約1170年にNigel de Mowbrayと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1204年にイギリスノーフォルクブレックランドで56歳で死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアコクスウオルドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Mabel de Clare(은)는 1148년 영국 링코실르에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1170년 Nigel de Mowbray(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1204년 영국 노르폴크 브레클랜드에서 56세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 콕스울드에 묻혔다.

mn.Epworth, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino: Epworth, Lindon, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Mabel de Clare nasceu em 1148, em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Nigel de Mowbray aproximadamente 1170. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1204, em Breckland, Norfolk, Inglaterra, com 56 anos, e foi sepultada em Coxwold, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Mabel de Clare родилась в 1148 г. в местности линколншир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Nigel de Mowbray около 1170 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1204 г. в местности бреккланд, норфолк, Англия в возрасте 56 лет и была похоронена в местности коксволд, еркшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Mabel de Clare nació en 1148, en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Se casó con Nigel de Mowbray aproximadamente 1170. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1204, en Breckland, Norfolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 56 años, y fue sepultada en Coxwold, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
